name: Talus
description_short: Talus is a full-service payment processing and fintech platform that provides omnichannel payment solutions, merchant onboarding, and gateway services to software companies, resellers, and SMBs.
description_long:
Talus is a financial technology company positioned as a Full-Service Provider (FSP) in the payment processing space. The company processes over $12 billion annually for 22,000+ merchants and maintains a 99.99% uptime guarantee. Founded to serve software companies, resellers, and small to mid-sized businesses, Talus provides an end-to-end payment infrastructure that consolidates multiple vendor relationships into a single integrated platform.

Talus offers a comprehensive suite of payment solutions centered around their proprietary Talus Connect gateway. Services include merchant onboarding, in-store and e-commerce payment acceptance, mobile and contactless payments, subscription/recurring billing, e-invoicing, email/text-to-pay, batch processing, POS equipment, developer tools, fraud detection, settlement, and reporting. The platform is designed to be brandable and embeddable, allowing software companies to white-label solutions within their own applications. They serve merchants across multiple industries and provide dedicated support without relying on third-party call centers.

Talus distinguishes itself through transparent pricing with no hidden fees, no long-term contracts, and the ability to cancel anytime. Every merchant receives individual underwriting and pricing based on their unique risk profile. The company emphasizes U.S.-based customer support available 24/7, AI-powered fraud detection and real-time monitoring, industry-leading security and compliance solutions, and dedicated merchant accounts (rather than pooled-risk aggregation). Their reseller and partner program offers white-label capabilities with residual compensation.

Talus appears well-established and trustworthy based on customer testimonials from banking partners and merchants citing excellent service, transparency, and responsiveness. However, as a payment processor rather than a lender, Talus does not provide traditional business financing or loans—it is a service provider for payment infrastructure. Merchants seeking capital for business growth would need to look elsewhere, though Talus enables them to monetize existing payment volume more effectively.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Proprietary omnichannel gateway (Talus Connect) supporting 10+ payment methods and channels
No hidden fees, no long-term contracts, cancelable agreements with transparent pricing
24/7 U.S.-based customer support from payments-expert employees, not offshore call centers
Dedicated merchant accounts for each customer rather than risky pooled aggregation
AI-powered real-time fraud detection and risk management with financial protection
White-label and embeddable solutions for software companies and resellers
Processes $12+ billion annually with 99.99% uptime SLA across 22,000+ merchants
cons: Not a traditional lender—Talus provides payment processing services, not business loans or capital financing
Pricing details are not publicly disclosed on website; merchants must undergo underwriting for quotes
Limited transparency on specific processing fees, interchange rates, or how pricing compares to competitors
Requires integration and onboarding, which may involve setup time and technical resources
best_for: Software companies seeking to embed white-label payment processing into their platforms
Resellers and referral partners wanting to offer payment solutions with transparent residuals
Small and mid-sized merchants prioritizing transparent pricing and U.S.-based support over long-term contracts
Businesses requiring omnichannel payment acceptance across retail, e-commerce, mobile, and subscription billing
diagnosis: Talus is designed for merchants, software companies, and resellers seeking transparent, consolidated payment processing infrastructure rather than traditional business financing. The main caveat is that Talus provides payment processing services, not loans or capital—businesses needing working capital or funding should explore dedicated small business loan lenders, though Talus enables them to monetize payment processing more efficiently.
services: Developer APIs and software integration tools
Real-time fraud detection and AI-powered risk management
Reseller and partner program with white-label capabilities
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
